metadata.title: Why the Group That Learned Less Was Sure It Learned More
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer build of Reallocation Engine candidate 01 — the fluency trap / comprehension debt. Source chapter: books/the-reallocation-engine/chapters/01-the-fluency-trap.md
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated archival plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation rings used once per graphic at most
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, dusty blue vs terracotta as the two group accents (blue = checking, terracotta = delegating), gold highlighter as the single editor's-pen voice

metadata.color_semantics: dusty blue #5B7B9C = hand-coders / interrogation / checking; terracotta #D35F43 = AI-delegation; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Counts to verify: hand-coders 67% vs AI group 50% (17 pts) on 14-question comprehension quiz; within AI group interrogation 65-86% vs delegation 24-39%. Source: Shen & Tamkin, 'How AI Impacts Skill Formation', arXiv:2601.20245 (v1 Jan 28 2026, v2 Feb 1 2026); Anthropic research post Jan 29 2026. Verified against both 2026-07-06 — see FACTCHECK.md. Card exclusions: no n=52/randomization mechanics, no boost/drag terminology, no IBM story, no OPT clock.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Two groups of engineers. The same problem, the same clock. One group walked away two letter grades dumber — and feeling smarter.

card.copy: Why the group that learned less was sure it learned more

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] Why the group that learned less was sure it learned more

beats.narration_text: A research lab took a group of junior engineers and handed them a Python library none of them had ever seen.
beats.scene_description: archival photograph of programmers at terminals in an open office, desaturated, seated on the newsprint page with cream border; slow push-in
beats.image_prompt: archival photograph of young software engineers at computer workstations in an office, 1980s-90s print reproduction, desaturated on aged newsprint

beats.narration_text: Half got an AI assistant. Half had to write every line by hand. Same problem, same clock — exactly one difference: who did the typing.

production_viz.label: one cohort, two conditions
production_viz.note: one square grid divides into terracotta and blue halves on 'Half'; no counts shown (exclusion: no n=52)

beats.new_visual_element: [ISOTYPE] cohort splits into terracotta and blue halves

beats.narration_text: The AI group finished clean. The code ran. The functions had comments. Everything on the screen looked like competence.
beats.scene_description: a code editor page seated on the newsprint ground, lines of tidy commented code scrolling into place, a green check appearing — treated to print desaturation
beats.image_prompt: computer screen with tidy commented Python code and a passing test indicator, flat print reproduction on aged newsprint, desaturated editorial collage
beats.video_prompt: code lines type themselves rapidly and settle, a small check mark stamps in the corner, subtle paper-plane parallax, no camera move
beats.new_visual_element: [FOOTAGE] clean code assembling itself, check mark

beats.narration_text: Then the twist. Everyone took the same quiz afterward. Not 'can you produce the code' — 'do you understand what the code does?'
beats.scene_description: a quiz paper set large in charcoal serif on the newsprint ground; golden highlighter bar sweeps 'understand' as it is spoken

document.quote: Do you understand what the code does?

beats.narration_text: The hand-coders averaged sixty-seven percent. The AI group, fifty. Seventeen points — nearly two letter grades — opened up in one short session on the very same problem.
And here's the thing — the AI group felt fine. The work was done. Nothing had failed. The trap never looks like failure.
beats.scene_description: the clean-code plate from B04 returns smaller, pinned to the page; a hand-drawn terracotta ring circles the green check mark on 'felt fine' — the editor's pen questioning the checkmark
beats.image_prompt: printed screenshot of tidy code with a bold check mark, pinned like a clipping to aged newsprint, desaturated flat print
beats.new_visual_element: [ANNOTATE] terracotta ring around the check mark

beats.narration_text: Because here's the mechanism: understanding doesn't form while the code gets typed. It forms while it gets checked — read, tested, questioned, broken, fixed.

production_viz.label: where understanding forms
production_viz.note: typing node drawn small and grey off to the side; the blue cycle draws itself stage by stage timed to the spoken verbs

beats.new_visual_element: [DIAGRAM] the checking loop draws itself; typing sits outside it

beats.narration_text: Look inside the AI group and the outcome splits cleanly. The engineers who interrogated the model — challenged it, asked why, made it explain — landed right beside the hand-coders.

production_viz.label: the variable is the checking, not the typing
production_viz.reference_line: faint dashed ink line at 67 crossing the interrogation band — the convergence IS the claim (chapter fig 1.2)
production_viz.note: verify: interrogation usage 65-86% contains 67; the two high columns share a behavior (checking), not a typing method

beats.new_visual_element: [CHART] B06's chart returns — interrogation band 65-86 rises beside the 67 bar, reference line joins them (chapter fig 1.2)

viz.label: interrogated 65-86, converging with hand-coding 67
viz.note: verify range 65-86; convergence with 67 is the point

beats.narration_text: The engineers who delegated — prompt, accept, move on — scored in the twenties and thirties. Same tool. Same task. Opposite result.
beats.scene_description: the third column drops in: AI + delegation, terracotta band 24-39, far below the two converged high columns; on 'Opposite result' a golden highlighter bar sweeps the empty vertical band between 39 and 65 in the delegation column

production_viz.note: verify: delegation usage 24-39%; the two high columns persist from B09 — the drop-off reads against them (chapter fig 1.2)

annotation.target: the gap between the two ranges

beats.new_visual_element: [CHART+ANNOTATE] delegation bar 24-39; gold bar sweeps the gap

beats.narration_text: So skipping the checking doesn't skip the cost of learning. It defers it. Every clean artifact you can't explain is a small loan you've taken out.

production_viz.label: comprehension debt
production_viz.mechanic: clean-artifact cards stamp onto a pile one per phrase; beside the pile a small ledger meter ticks upward in terracotta with each card
production_viz.note: metaphor beat — no numbers; the ledger has no scale, only growth

beats.new_visual_element: [ISOTYPE] artifact cards pile up; terracotta debt meter ticks

beats.narration_text: Comprehension debt compounds quietly — right up until the system breaks in a way that requires you to understand it. The loan comes due. With interest.
beats.scene_description: a red error page plate seated on the newsprint over the ledger from B11; the terracotta debt meter from B11 persists on the annotation plane and stamps 'DUE' on 'comes due'
beats.image_prompt: computer screen filled with a stack-trace error page, harsh and dense, printed reproduction desaturated on aged newsprint, editorial collage
beats.new_visual_element: [PLATE+ANNOTATE] the break arrives; DUE stamp lands

beats.narration_text: The machine is a better typist than you will ever be. Let it type. But understanding forms during the checking — and the checking was never the machine's job. Keep it.

card.copy: Let it type. Keep the checking.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — Let it type. Keep the checking.
